title: Powers of fractions & decimals
description: Practice taking positive whole-number powers of fractions and decimals.

hints: $\left(\dfrac12\right)^3=\dfrac{1}{8}$
$\left(\dfrac13\right)^3=\dfrac{1}{27}$
$\left(\dfrac14\right)^2=\dfrac{1}{16}$
$\left(\dfrac15\right)^2=\dfrac{1}{25}$
$\left(\dfrac32\right)^2=\dfrac{27}{8}$
$\left(\dfrac25\right)^2=\dfrac{4}{25}$
$\left(\dfrac47\right)^2=\dfrac{16}{49}$

An exponent tells us how many factors of the base we should multiply to find the value of an expression.

For example, we can take the power of a decimal like this:

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{0.5}^\blueE{3} &= \maroonD{0.5} \cdot \maroonD{0.5} \cdot \maroonD{0.5}
\\\\
&=0.125
\end{align}$

An exponent tells us how many factors of the base we should multiply to find the value of an expression.
